vBRIEFInfo.author: panopticon-cli/0.0.0
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1429: TTS first-utterance clip regressed for DisplayPort/HDMI sinks (PAN-1028 covered USB DAC only)

plan.title: TTS first-utterance clip regressed for DisplayPort/HDMI sinks (PAN-1028 covered USB DAC only)
plan.author: agent:claude-opus-4-7

narratives.Problem: PAN-1028 added a 600ms silence prepend on SUSPENDED-sink cold starts, calibrated for PipeWire+USB DAC resume latency (50-500ms). DisplayPort/HDMI audio sinks (e.g. GA102 HDMI 2) take 1.0-1.5s to wake, so the first ~500ms of the actual phrase still gets clipped on cold starts. Interim commit 0ac7ae801 raised the prepend to a blanket 1.5s, but that imposes 900ms of unnecessary leading silence on the USB DAC path that PAN-1028 originally tuned for.
narratives.Proposal: Replace the single SUSPENDED-vs-RUNNING prepend constant with a sink-kind-aware mapping. Probe `pactl list sinks` long-form once per cold start to classify the default sink as one of usb / hdmi / bluetooth / unknown, and pick the prepend from a table: usb=600ms (preserves PAN-1028 behaviour), hdmi=1500ms (covers DisplayPort/HDMI), bluetooth=1000ms, unknown=1500ms (safe default). Warm-path stays at 50ms with zero pactl overhead. Add a stdlib-unittest test that asserts the kind→prepend mapping with mocked pactl fixtures, and update the pan-tts SKILL.md PipeWire Suspend section.

autoDecisions.summary: Chose the sink-type-aware approach over bumping PLAYER_IDLE_TIMEOUT.
autoDecisions.rationale: Acceptance criterion 3 explicitly requires 'Unit test asserts sink-type → prepend mapping', which only makes sense for the sink-type-aware fix. The PLAYER_IDLE_TIMEOUT alternative would also keep a pw-play subprocess permanently bound to the sink, which conflicts with the existing 'sink returns to SUSPENDED within a reasonable timeout' contract documented in SKILL.md.

autoDecisions.summary: Used the prepend values listed in the issue body verbatim: usb=600ms, hdmi=1500ms, bluetooth=1000ms, unknown=1500ms.
autoDecisions.rationale: These match the hardware-variance table in the issue, preserve PAN-1028's tuned USB DAC value, and pick the safe upper bound for the unknown bucket.

autoDecisions.summary: Merged sink-state probe and sink-kind probe into a single `pactl list sinks` long-form call.
autoDecisions.rationale: The current `_get_default_sink_state()` already runs two pactl invocations (`pactl info` + `pactl list short sinks`). Long-form `pactl list sinks` carries both the State and Properties/Driver fields needed for classification, so one call replaces two and avoids regressing cold-start latency. Falls back to (SUSPENDED, unknown) → 1500ms safe default on any failure.

autoDecisions.summary: Treated DisplayPort sinks as part of the HDMI bucket (single 1500ms value).
autoDecisions.rationale: DisplayPort audio routes through the same HDMI-class GPU codec on Linux; both share the 1.0-1.5s wake-from-suspend characteristic described in the issue.

autoDecisions.summary: Tests use stdlib `unittest` + `unittest.mock`, runnable via `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests`.
autoDecisions.rationale: Repo has no existing Python test runner (no pyproject.toml, pytest.ini, or setup.cfg under sync-sources/). stdlib unittest avoids introducing a dependency the daemon's bundled CUDA venv would have to track.

autoDecisions.summary: Edits target sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/ only; the .claude/skills/pan-tts/ mirror is regenerated by `pan sync` and not edited directly.
autoDecisions.rationale: The interim commit 0ac7ae801 followed this convention. Editing the mirror directly would create drift and conflict with the next `pan sync` run.

autoDecisions.summary: Did not introduce a config knob to override the prepend table.
autoDecisions.rationale: Issue scope is regression repair, not configurability. A future PR can expose `tts.prependSecs.<kind>` if needed; encoding it now would balloon scope and require dashboard / config schema changes.

items.title: Add sink-kind probe and apply kind-aware prepend in play_audio()

narrative.Action: In sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py: (a) introduce a module-level SINK_PREPEND_SECS = {'usb': 0.60, 'hdmi': 1.50, 'bluetooth': 1.00, 'unknown': 1.50} mapping; (b) replace _get_default_sink_state() with _probe_default_sink() returning a (state, kind) tuple parsed from a single `pactl list sinks` (long-form) call — classify kind by Driver, Properties (device.bus, device.class, api.bluez5.transport), and Description keywords (HDMI / DisplayPort / DP / USB / Bluetooth); fall back to (state='SUSPENDED', kind='unknown') on any subprocess or parse error; (c) in play_audio(), when `created` is True call the new probe and pick prepend_secs from SINK_PREPEND_SECS[kind] for SUSPENDED sinks, retain 0.05s for RUNNING sinks and warm pw-play reuse; (d) replace the PAN-1028 inline comment block with a short pointer to SINK_PREPEND_SECS. Do NOT edit .claude/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py — that file is the sync mirror.

subItems.title: First TTS utterance after sink suspend is not clipped on DisplayPort/HDMI hardware (sink kind 'hdmi' yields 1500ms prepend).
Existing USB DAC behaviour preserved: sink kind 'usb' yields 600ms prepend (the original PAN-1028 value), not 1500ms.
Bluetooth sinks get 1000ms prepend; unrecognised sinks get the safe 1500ms default.
Warm-path prepend (RUNNING sink or reused pw-play subprocess) is unchanged at 50ms and does NOT call pactl.
Probe failures (pactl missing, non-zero return, parse error, timeout) return (SUSPENDED, unknown) so the 1500ms safe default applies — the daemon never raises into the playback path.

Only sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py is edited; .claude/skills/pan-tts/scripts/tts_daemon.py is left for `pan sync` to regenerate (or regenerated in the same commit via the sync hook).

items.title: Add stdlib-unittest tests for sink-kind detection and prepend mapping

narrative.Action: Create sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests/test_tts_daemon.py using stdlib `unittest` and `unittest.mock.patch`. Patch `subprocess.run` to return canned `pactl list sinks` fixtures for each sink kind (USB DAC, HDMI/GPU, DisplayPort/GPU, Bluetooth A2DP, unknown/empty output, FileNotFoundError). Assert that `_probe_default_sink()` returns the correct (state, kind) tuple AND that `SINK_PREPEND_SECS[kind]` yields the expected prepend duration for each fixture. Also add a `tests/__init__.py` if needed so `python -m unittest discover` finds the module. Do not depend on pytest or any third-party package. Tests must run cleanly from the workspace root with `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests`.

subItems.title: test_tts_daemon.py exists at sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests/test_tts_daemon.py and is runnable with `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests` from the workspace root, no third-party deps required.
Test covers each sink kind (usb, hdmi, bluetooth, unknown) by patching subprocess.run with a realistic `pactl list sinks` long-form fixture and asserting the returned kind.
Test covers the DisplayPort case explicitly (a fixture whose Description mentions 'DisplayPort' or 'DP' rather than only 'HDMI') and asserts kind classification still resolves to 'hdmi'.
Test covers probe-failure paths (pactl FileNotFoundError, non-zero return, timeout) and asserts they resolve to (SUSPENDED, 'unknown') → 1500ms prepend.
Test asserts the full SINK_PREPEND_SECS mapping numerically: usb=0.60, hdmi=1.50, bluetooth=1.00, unknown=1.50.
All new tests pass under `python -m unittest discover sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/tests` — zero failures, zero skips that hide AC coverage.

items.title: Update pan-tts SKILL.md PipeWire Suspend section with the kind→prepend table

narrative.Action: In sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md under 'PipeWire Suspend', replace the single '600ms of silence is prepended on cold starts' sentence with a short paragraph plus a small table covering the kind→prepend mapping (USB DAC 600ms, HDMI/DisplayPort 1500ms, Bluetooth 1000ms, unknown 1500ms). Cite PAN-1028 (USB DAC origin) and PAN-1429 (this fix). Note the trade-off recorded in hazard H5 (Bluetooth latency is codec-dependent; 1000ms is a baseline). Do not edit the .claude/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md mirror.

subItems.title: SKILL.md PipeWire Suspend section documents the four sink-kind buckets with their prepend durations in a readable form (table or bullet list).
Doc references both PAN-1028 (USB DAC origin) and PAN-1429 (DisplayPort/HDMI extension) so future readers can trace the history.
Doc notes that the probe only runs on cold start (sink SUSPENDED + freshly spawned pw-play) and that the warm path stays at 50ms with no pactl invocation.
Only sync-sources/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md is edited; .claude/skills/pan-tts/SKILL.md is left for `pan sync` to regenerate.
